<!--
Organization-wide template. Do not edit this file in place for a specific release.

How to use:
1. Copy this file into your project repository.
2. Save it as docs/releases/<release-slug>.md using a lowercase kebab-case slug
   (for example: alpha, v0-1).
3. Replace placeholders and remove this comment block.
4. Create milestone documents under docs/milestones/ for each vertical slice.

Actual release definitions live in each project repository, not in the org
.github repository.

For workflow context, see the PathAble Open Source Operating Model:
https://github.com/PathableAI-org/.github/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#open-source-operating-model

Naming: docs/releases/<release-slug>.md
-->

# <Release name>

**Status:** Draft | In progress | Shipped

This document is the public planning contract for a planned release. When the
release ships, summarize what actually shipped in a
[GitHub Release](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/releasing-projects-on-github)
and link back to this definition.
